note: IB Diploma Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation HL, first assessment 2021 (valid 2021-2027). Five syllabus topics (1 Number & algebra, 2 Functions, 3 Geometry & trigonometry, 4 Statistics & probability, 5 Calculus). AI emphasises modelling, technology and real-world applications (matrices, eigenvalues, Voronoi diagrams, graph theory, Markov chains, hypothesis testing) over AA's pure proof/algebra. Lessons are curated concept clusters across SL + Additional Higher Level content; codes 1.1-5.9 use topic.cluster numbering and sort in syllabus order. Factual syllabus structure only.

topics.name: Standard form, approximation and percentage error
Arithmetic and geometric sequences and series
Financial mathematics: compound interest, annuities and amortization
Exponents and logarithms
Systems of equations and matrix operations
Matrices: determinants, inverses and applications
Eigenvalues and eigenvectors
Complex numbers: Cartesian and polar form for applications
Linear models and equations of straight lines
Functions: domain, range, inverse and graphs
Modelling with quadratic, cubic and power functions
Modelling with exponential and logarithmic functions
Modelling with sinusoidal functions
Modelling with logistic, piecewise and other functions
Linearizing data: log-log and semi-log graphs
Distances, volumes and surface areas of 3D solids
Trigonometry: sine and cosine rules
Applications: bearings, elevation and gradients
Voronoi diagrams and nearest-neighbour problems
Vectors: properties, operations and applications
Vector applications: kinematics and motion
Graph theory: adjacency matrices and walks
Graph algorithms: spanning trees, Chinese postman and travelling salesman
Sampling techniques and data collection
Descriptive statistics
Correlation and regression: Pearson and Spearman
Probability, Venn diagrams and tree diagrams
Discrete random variables and expected value
Binomial and Poisson distributions
The normal distribution
Hypothesis testing: chi-squared and t-tests
Confidence intervals and further hypothesis testing
Transition matrices and Markov chains
Introduction to differentiation and rates of change
Differentiation: tangents, normals and optimization
Introduction to integration and area under a curve
The trapezoidal rule and numerical integration
Further differentiation and the second derivative
Definite integrals: areas and volumes of revolution
Kinematics and related rates of change
Differential equations: Euler's method and slope fields
Coupled and second-order differential equations
